confirmed.title: LineChart Y-axis ticks and tooltip values not locale-formatted
confirmed.detail: <YAxis /> (line 21) and <Tooltip /> (line 22) are rendered with no `tickFormatter`/`formatter`, so recharts falls back to its default numeric rendering (effectively String(value)) with no locale thousands grouping or decimal separator. A pt-BR/de/fr user viewing a dataset of e.g. 1500000 sees `1500000` on the Y axis and tooltip instead of `1.500.000`. The component imports no locale (no useArqelLocale) and the chart data carries only raw number[] (types.ts ChartDataset.data: number[]), so there is no locale plumbing into the axis/tooltip at all. Repro: register a Chart widget with a line dataset containing a large number, set app locale to pt_BR, render — axis/tooltip digits are ungrouped en-style. Fix: thread the active locale (useArqelLocale) and pass tickFormatter={(v)=>new Intl.NumberFormat(locale).format(v)} to YAxis plus a tooltip formatter.
confirmed.why_real:
VERIFIED REAL, NOT DUPLICATE. Read packages-js/ui/src/widgets/charts/LineChart.tsx: line 21 renders <YAxis /> and line 22 <Tooltip /> with no tickFormatter/formatter. Recharts falls back to String(value), so a pt-BR/de/fr user viewing a large value (e.g. 1500000) sees ungrouped en-style digits instead of 1.500.000. The component imports no locale hook, so there is zero locale plumbing into the axis/tooltip. This is genuinely user-facing.

Defect class is established and in-scope: Round 9 of the ledger fixed the directly analogous 'StatCard numeric KPI value rendered raw without Intl.NumberFormat grouping' using the exact suggested fix (const locale = useArqelLocale(); new Intl.NumberFormat(locale).format(value)). The useArqelLocale hook from @arqel-dev/react/utils exists and is already used in StatCard.tsx:110-111, cells.tsx, TablePagination.tsx, TableCard.tsx — so the fix is trivially plumbable.

NOT a duplicate: scanned the full 337-entry R1-R26 ledger (max round 26). No chart axis/tooltip/tickFormatter entry exists for any widgets/charts/*.tsx file. The only chart-related ledger entry is ChartCard.tsx 'Loading chart' aria-label (Round 1), which is unrelated.

NOT vendored: path is packages-js/ui/src/widgets/charts/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

Caveat (lowers confidence on scope, not on realness): the identical unformatted YAxis/Tooltip defect also exists in sibling BarChart.tsx (lines 21-22), AreaChart.tsx (lines 21-22), RadarChart.tsx, and PieChart.tsx — an un-swept family of 5+ chart files. The finding as written correctly identifies LineChart as one real, novel, reproducible instance.

confirmed.title: AreaChart Y-axis ticks and tooltip values not locale-formatted
confirmed.detail: Same gap as LineChart: <YAxis /> (line 21) and <Tooltip /> (line 22) have no tickFormatter/formatter, so numeric axis ticks and tooltip values render via recharts' default (raw, ungrouped) instead of through Intl.NumberFormat in the active locale. No useArqelLocale import; ChartSubProps carries no locale. Repro: area chart with value 1234567 under pt_BR shows `1234567` not `1.234.567`. Fix: add a locale-aware tickFormatter + tooltip formatter.
confirmed.why_real:
Code state verified at packages-js/ui/src/widgets/charts/AreaChart.tsx:21-22: bare <YAxis /> and <Tooltip /> with no tickFormatter/formatter, no useArqelLocale import, and ChartSubProps (widgets/types.ts) carries no locale. Numeric axis ticks and tooltip values therefore render via recharts' default raw/ungrouped formatting.

This is a genuine i18n defect, not a greenfield enhancement, because the framework already establishes a numeric-locale-formatting contract elsewhere: StatCard.tsx:110-118 formats numbers via `new Intl.NumberFormat(locale)` with the exact documented behavior `1234567 → 1.234.567` under pt-BR; TableCard.tsx and table/cells.tsx use useArqelLocale + Intl.NumberFormat the same way. So chart axis/tooltip numbers being raw is an inconsistency with the framework's own active-locale number formatting — user-facing (a pt-BR user sees `1234567` in chart ticks/tooltip while the same value is `1.234.567` in StatCards/tables). Repro is valid.

Ledger check (reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json, rounds 1-27): no chart locale-format / tickFormatter / YAxis / Tooltip entry for any chart exists. Closest entries are ChartCard.tsx aria-label (Loading chart) and a demo dashboard ->format('M j') month abbreviation — both unrelated. Not a duplicate.

Not vendored: file is under widgets/charts/, not shadcn/*.

Caveat lowering confidence: the defect is systemic and identical across LineChart, AreaChart, BarChart, and RadarChart (all have bare <YAxis/>/<Tooltip/>); the finding itself notes 'Same gap as LineChart.' It is a defect-family reported per-file. Since no chart-locale entry is in the ledger, AreaChart is not a duplicate of any recorded item, but it is one slice of a multi-file family.

confirmed.title: PieChart/DonutChart slice values not locale-formatted and hardcoded English 'Slice N' fallback label
confirmed.detail: Two issues. (1) locale-format: the `label` prop on <Pie> (line 39) and <Tooltip /> (line 45) render the raw numeric `value` with no formatter, so slice value labels/tooltips show ungrouped en-style digits regardless of locale (e.g. 1200000 not 1.200.000 under pt_BR). No useArqelLocale import; ChartSubProps carries no locale. (2) missing-i18n: line 25 uses a hardcoded English fallback name `Slice ${i + 1}` when chartData.labels[i] is absent, so an unlabeled slice always reads 'Slice 1' even under pt_BR. DonutChart delegates entirely to PieChart so it inherits both. Repro: pie widget with fewer labels than data points under pt_BR — fallback slice reads 'Slice 2' and its value label is ungrouped. Fix: route value through Intl.NumberFormat(locale) in a Pie label/tooltip formatter, and replace the literal with a translated key like arqel::chart.slice_fallback with a :number placeholder.
confirmed.why_real: Verified against /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/ui/src/widgets/charts/PieChart.tsx. Both sub-claims reproduce. (1) locale-format: line 39 `label` on <Pie> and line 45 <Tooltip /> render raw numeric slice values; there is no useArqelLocale import and ChartSubProps (types.ts:29) carries no locale, so values display ungrouped en-style digits under pt_BR (1200000 not 1.200.000). The same package proves this is a real gap by formatting numbers via useArqelLocale + Intl.NumberFormat in StatCard.tsx:110-111, TableCard.tsx:45-46, TablePagination.tsx:30-31 and cells.tsx:218/247. (2) missing-i18n: line 25 `chartData.labels[i] ?? `Slice ${i + 1}`` hardcodes an English fallback. types.ts confirms labels:string[] and datasets[0].data:number[] are independent arrays with no enforced length parity, so when data outnumbers labels an unlabeled slice renders literal 'Slice N' regardless of locale — the repro is valid. DonutChart.tsx delegates entirely to PieChart and inherits both defects. Not vendored: file lives in widgets/charts/, not shadcn/*; it is first-party framework code shipped to consumers. Not a duplicate: scanned all 336 ledger entries (rounds 1-27); nearest neighbors are StatCard raw-KPI R9, VersionTimeline RelativeTimeFormat R2, ChartCard aria-label R1 — none cover PieChart/DonutChart slice value formatting or the 'Slice N' fallback.
